title: Mount Allison University-Campus Nature Challenge 2025
description:
From Sept. 21 to Sept. 28, track biodiversity at Mt. Allison University during the 2025 Campus Nature Challenge, hosted by the Campus Biodiversity Network. For more information: campusnaturechallenge.org   This project also has a community aspect: Sackville Waterfowl Park, as a site of significance to the campus, will also be counted toward the Mt. Allison project.

To view the permanent Mount Allison Campus Biodiversity Network collection project: pending
To view the 2025 Campus Nature Challenge umbrella project: https://inaturalist.ca/projects/campus-nature-challenge-2025-defi-nature-campus

This project runs in conjunction with the Great Canadian Bioblitz, held in Canada between Sept. 21-28 and hosted by the Canadian Wildlife Federation. For more information on the Great Canadian Bioblitz, see https://inaturalist.ca/projects/the-cwf-great-canadian-bioblitz-le-grand-bioblitz-canadien-de-la-fcf

The Campus Nature Challenge is organized by the Campus Biodiversity Network, a consortium of universities and colleges around the world documenting biodiversity on university campuses. For more information on the network and to view ongoing observations on biodiversity at university and college campuses around the world, visit https://www.biodivcampus.org, https://www.definaturecampus.org, and https://www.inaturalist.ca/projects/campus-biodiversity-network-la-biodiversite-au-campus.

title: Collaboration Heritage College & Cégep de l'Outaouais- Campus Nature Challenge 2025
description:
Discover and track biodiversity on campus greenspace at Heritage College and Cégep de l'Outaouais in Gatineau, Québec, for the Campus Nature Challenge 2025, hosted by the Campus Biodiversity Network.  This project has a community component, namely the biodiversity corridor between the two campuses.  Find out more at definaturecampus.org et biodivcampus.org.

Découvrez et suivez la biodiversité dans les espaces verts du campus au Cégep Heritage College et du Cégep de l'Outaouais à Gatineau, Québec dans le cadre du Défi nature campus, organisé par le réseau Biodiversité au campus. Ce projet a également un aspect communautaire, notamment le corridor de biodiversité entre les deux cégeps.  Voir definaturecampus.org et biodivcampus.org pour plus d'information.

title: Bioblitz du Cégep de l'Outaouais - Septembre 2025
description:
Portrait de la biodiversité sur les campus Gabrielle-Roy et Félix-Leclerc en septembre 2025 avec la participation de notre communauté collégiale. Le Bioblitz du Cégep de l'Outaouais a lieu en même temps que le Défi Nautre Campus organisé par Biodiversité au Campus - campusnaturechallenge.org - et en même temps que le Grand Bioblitz canadien, organisé par la Fédération canadienne de la faune (FCF) - https://cwf-fcf.org/fr/explorer/inaturalist/bioblitz/

Rendez-vous le vendredi 26 septembre à 14h15 à l'Auditorium (Gabrielle-Roy) ou au F2040 (Félix-Leclerc) pour une séance d'information et l'activité principale.

title: McGill University - Campus Nature Challenge 2025
description:
Participate in the Campus Nature Challenge, hosted by the Campus Biodiversity Network, at McGill University. Please note that this project includes the downtown campus area, the Macdonald campus, as well as the Gault, Penfield and Molson reserves. See the full umbrella project for McGill's Bioblitz 2025 here  https://inaturalist.ca/projects/mcgill-university-s-bioblitz-2025

The Campus Biodiversity Network promotes the observation of biodiversity at higher-education institutions worldwide. Based in Quebec, Canada, it is open to all post-secondary institutions. To have your campus become a member of the network or for more information, please visit https://www.biodivcampus.org.

See McGill's permanent collection project, which is also included in the following network projects:
International umbrella project: https://www.inaturalist.ca/projects/campus-biodiversity-network-la-biodiversite-au-campus
Canada umbrella project: https://inaturalist.ca/projects/cbn-rbc-canada
Québec umbrella project: https://inaturalist.ca/projects/cbn-rbc-quebec

**McGill is a signatory to the Nature Positive Universities alliance. The Campus Biodiversity Network works in partnership with Oxford University's Nature Positive Universities Alliance and encourages higher-education institutions to become members. To become an NPU member or find out more about the Nature Positive Universities Alliance, visit https://naturepositiveuniversities.net

______

Participez au Défi nature sur le campus, et recensez la biodiversité sur les espaces verts du campus à l'Université de McGill du 21 au 28 septembre 2025.  Veuillez noter que ce projet contient le secteur du campus centre-ville,  le campus Macdonald, ainsi que la réserve Gault, et les réserves Penfield et Molson. 

Le réseau Biodiversité au campus vise à promouvoir l'observation de la biodiversité sur les campus des établissements post-secondaires dans le monde entier. Basé au Québec, Canada, et ouvert à tous les établissements d'éducation supérieure. Projet-cadre Québec: https://inaturalist.ca/projects/cbn-rbc-quebec

Le réseau Biodiversité au campus travaille en partenariat avec Nature Positive Universities Alliance de l'Université d'Oxford et encourage les établissements post-secondaires de devenir membre. Participate in the 2025 CBN Campus Nature Challenge, by documenting biodiversity on the campus of Université Laval from September 21-28, 2025.

In December 2022, Université Laval joined forces with 10 Quebec universities to protect biodiversity. Together, they will sign the Nature Positive pledge, becoming founders of an international movement involving 117 institutions around the world. title: Université de Sherbrooke-Défi nature campus 2025
description:
Participez au Défi nature campus en recensant un maximum d’espèces vivantes observées sur les campus universitaires via iNaturalist. Ce Défi prend place pendant le festival de biodiversité 2025 et le Grand Bioblitz Canadien de la Fédération Canadienne de la Faune.  Pour plus de détails sur le Défi nature campus: https://definaturecampus.org/

Vivants de nature
En 2025, l’Université de Sherbrooke organise pour la première fois une semaine thématique sur la biodiversité. Intitulé Vivants de nature, ce festival est une occasion pour la communauté universitaire, autant étudiante qu’employée, de se familiariser aux enjeux de la biodiversité de diverses manières. 

Ce projet découle du Plan d’action en Gestion environnementale 2023-2026, où une action visait à organiser une Journée biodiversité sur les campus afin de faire valoir les initiatives du plan d’action. Mais pourquoi s'arrêter à une seule journée? C’est toute une semaine qui vous est présentée pour animer les campus du 22 au 26 septembre 2025!

Pour plus de détails sur la programmation:  https://www.usherbrooke.ca/immeubles/gestion-environnementale/biodiversite/vivants-de-nature

description:

12h : Apportez votre lunch à la salle 2330-2332 de l'UQ - Siège social (475, rue du Parvis, Québec), on vous parlera de l'importance de la biodiversité et on vous expliquera comment fonctionne un bioblitz

title: Expedition Fiord Arctic Bioblitz
description: As part of Blitz the Gap 2025, the aim of this bioblitz is to augment the number of occurrence records of organisms in the Expedition Fiord region of Axel Heiberg Island (Inuktitut name: Umingmat Nunaat), an uninhabited island in the Qikiqtaaluk Region of Nunavut in the Canadian High Arctic.

title: Cypress Hills Bioblitz 2025
description:
Alberta Wilderness Associations (AWA), Nature Alberta, Alberta Native Bee Council (ANBC), Royal Alberta Museum and our partner organizations are hosting a Bioblitz at Cypress Hills Provincial Park on June 6-8, 2025. This event aims to collect information on the species that can be found in the park. Expert biologists and ecologists will participate, supporting the observation of known species through photographic, video and audio recordings, as well as sample colection of invertebrates.
A report summarizing our finding will be published after the event, and will be publicly available online at Albertawilderness.ca.
Participating experts include: 
Alyssa Metro, Alberta Amphibian and Reptile Conservancy
Brevan Wagner, Alberta Lepidopterists' Guild & University of Alberta
Dan L. Johnson, University of Lethbridge
Dana Bush, Alberta Native Plant Council
Hilary Pittel, Alberta Lepidopterists' Guild
Ilan Domnich, Alberta Native Bee Council
Rowan Rowland, University of Alberta
Tiffani Harrison, Alberta Native Bee Council and Metis Nation of Alberta

title: BioBlitz the Gap: Gault Nature Reserve
description: Come help us Blitz the Gap at Gault Nature Reserve on June 6th! Our mission? To get as close as possible to a complete species list in one day while recording our effort (time spent x number of observers). As a second goal, we aim to be Trailblazers and get away from the parking lot! Why? Gault Nature Reserve is one of the McGill University Field Stations that we, in the Biology Department, know and love. While Gault is relatively well sampled, there are still gaps! This challenge is being organized by the Quantitative Biodiversity Lab.

title: Forêt Ouareau BioBlitz 2025
description: The 2025 Soper lab BioBlitz outing, aiming to observe as many different species as possible in Ouareau Forest! Additionally, we are trying to meet as many of the challenges put forth to Blitz the Gap as much as possible.

title: BioBlitz the Gap: CSEE Île du marais
description: A place to log iNat records for the CSEE field trip to Île du marais

title: Blitz the Gap: Quebec soil fauna
description:
This lab bioblitz aims to identify long-forgotten samples under microscope as part of the 2025 Blitz the Gap initiative with the support of QCBS! Our research lab (Handa, UQÀM) aims to increase documentation of Quebec soil biodiversity by identifying as much organisms as possible (mostly mites, springtails and spiders) from samples collected in the last 10 years across various regions, whether with pitfall traps, soil cores or hand picking.

Note: as they fit the general taxonomic criteria of the project, few species seen on the field are counted in our observations despite not be living in the soil per se. We will do a clean up eventually by excluding them specifically.

title: BioBlitz the Gap: CSEE Johnville Bog & Forest Park
description: A place for logging iNat records during the CSEE field trip to Johnville Bog & Forest Park.

title: BioBlitz the Gap: CSEE Parc national du Mont-Mégantic
description: A place for logging iNat records during the CSEE field trip to Parc national du Mont-Mégantic.

title: Ram Mountain biodiversity blitz 2025
description: The primary objective of this project is to establish an inventory of the biodiversity of Ram Mountain

title: UBC Farm Bioblitz - Blitz the Gap!
description: This project is designed for bioblitzes at the UBC Farm from 2025 onward. Observations will contribute to the Blitz the Gap project in an effort to fill gaps in biodiversity knowledge across Canada. Observations will automatically be added to the project. Observations of any organisms encouraged!

title: Bamfield Spider-Blitz 2025
description:
A project to track the observations from the Bamfield Spider-blitz. This project was made possible by the Canadian Wildlife Federation and is part of their Great Canadian Bioblitz. (Full project here: https://www.inaturalist.org/projects/the-cwf-great-canadian-bioblitz-le-grand-bioblitz-canadien-de-la-fcf )

While arachnids are the focus of our blitz, we are easily distracted and enjoy observing all kinds of taxa.

title: CWF/FCF - WCS Great Canadian BatBlitz 2025
description: A compilation of observations made by BatBlitz volunteers from Wildlife Conservation Society (WCS) Canada and partners, during [The CWF Great Canadian BioBlitz](https://inaturalist.ca/projects/the-cwf-great-canadian-bioblitz-le-grand-bioblitz-canadien-de-la-fcf). The BatBlitz was held between Sept. 21- 28th, with 5 nights of acoustic recording, and two nights of targeted captures, supported by CWF. This Batblitz sought to inventory bat species in B.C., which are underrepresented in iNaturalist observations yet significantly diverse throughout the province.

title: Cortes Island  2025 Northern Wilds Bioblitz
description: This project is to document the spectacular biological diversity of Cortes Island’s Northern Wilds. This bioblitz is organized with the support of The Canadian Wildlife Federation as a part of the Great Canadian Bioblitz for 2025.

title: Marais de la Rivière-aux-Cerises
description: L’Association-du-Marais-de-la-Rivière-aux-Cerises (LAMRAC) est un organisme sans but lucratif fondé en 1997 et dont la mission est de protéger et de mettre en valeur le territoire du Marais de la Rivière-aux-Cerises, situé en plein coeur de Magog. Sur un territoire de plus de 180 hectares, tourbière, marécages, marais et boisés s’enlacent et génèrent une biodiversité exceptionnelle dont plus de 250 000 visiteurs, petits et grands, profitent annuellement. Ce projet vise à mettre à jour la liste d'espèces présentes sur le territoire du Marais et à suivre son évolution dans le temps. Ainsi, les citoyens de Magog, visiteurs du parc, les gestionnaires du territoire et les projets de recherche pourront bénéficier de cette riche banque d'information.

title: City of Edmonton - Great Canadian Bioblitz
description:
Join the City of Edmonton's community science project as part of the Canadian Wildlife Federation's Great Canadian Bioblitz, hosted by the Invasive Plant Outreach team, and sponsored by CWF! We have some great prizes for participants who record the most observations, the most species and the most invasive plants (regulated under the Alberta Weed Control Act) during the September 21-28 window.

Join this project to be considered for prizes. Observations can be made from September 21 - 28, 2025 within the City of Edmonton. You will have until October 5, 2025 to upload all media. Winners will be decided and contacted via iNaturalist by the project admin, fionaspitzig. We will announce all stats in our November IPM newsletter (subscribe at https://edmonton.us20.list-manage.com/subscribe?u=9e8180070e4d722409d4b1d68&id=2db321bdd5) 

Learn more about the CWF Great Canadian Bioblitz below: 

The CWF Great Canadian Bioblitz returns to provide nature enthusiasts with a fun and easy way to connect with nature — and take inventory of Canada’s wildlife species.

Your goal for participation — to record as many different species as possible of flora, funga and fauna you can find over the course of a weeklong open invitation event. Gather friends, family, nature lovers, students or photography buffs and carve off a few hours to explore the outdoors in your area, (be it your backyard, city parks, or local wilderness area).

Wherever you are in Canada, snap photos or capture sound recordings of everything you find — from iconic wildlife to tiny insects, from birds, bugs and bees to wild plants and trees. Every species counts, from the common to the rare, but extra kudos to people who venture to a new area or hone in on a more unique and harder to find species.

Upload every observation using the free iNaturalist app or online at iNaturalist.ca. You’ll be helping us take a “nature selfie” of Canada. You will also be contributing to an active database scientists use to track and conserve biodiversity across Canada and around the world.

title: Foray NL Mushroom & Lichen Diversity
description: This Foray NL "Collection" project aims to document the fungal diversity of Newfoundland and Labrador.  We hope this resource will be useful for everyone, from those who are new to learning about fungi, to the seasoned forager, to the global research community.  No need to "tag" us or join this project; if you meet project requirements (minimum requirements: Kingdom Fungi and lower; good photos, locations in NL) your observations will be included automatically.  Happy Hunting Everyone!

title: Swan Lake Christmas Hill Nature Sanctuary
description:
Swan Lake Christmas Hill Nature Sanctuary is comprised of 160+ acres divided into two physically and ecologically distinct areas – a low wetland area surrounding Swan Lake, and a rocky Garry Oak-forested hilltop on Christmas Hill. 

The 48 acre Swan Lake area includes streams, small forested areas, woody shrubs and large areas of grass. During the winter months, the lake and flooded fields provide resting and feeding areas for a variety of grebes, ducks, and other waterfowl. In spring and summer, the forests and blooming hedgerows are filled with nesting birds such as warblers, sparrows, wrens, and finches. The fields of cattails and marsh grass are alive with calling red-winged blackbirds and marsh wrens. Muskrat, river otter and mink make their homes in the rock walls and quarry beside the lake and are found here year round. The threatened Western Painted Turtle can be seen in and around the lake.

Christmas Hill includes diverse assemblages of some of the region’s rarest plant communities, including deep-soil Douglas fir and Garry oak woodland, rock outcrop, and vernal pond communities. The Hill is home to approximately 250 plant species and eight plant species at risk. Over time, natural succession has changed the environmental structure of many of the lower elevation ecosystems from open woodlands and meadows to forested thickets. These ecological changes threaten the continued growth of some meadow species as well as some plant species at risk.

Description sourced from www.swanlake.bc.ca

title: Bug Battle - 2025 - Kings County Count Week
description:
Calling all community scientists, aspiring entomologists, lovers of creepy-crawlies, and overall bug enthusiasts, now is your time to shine! 

Introducing the 2025 Bug Battle! A collaborative community competition between Annapolis and Kings County to determine which county can collect the most species of bugs (arthropods and worms). This is a week-long competition; all observations within county boundaries will be counted. Please respect current DNR restrictions on travel (https://novascotia.ca/burnsafe/)


Join us for our in-person event on September 27th! 
We want to celebrate all of your valuable contributions. As a thank you, there will be prize packs awarded to the iNatters with the most observations, plus giveaways and prize draws for participating. Come bug out with us!
Date: September 27, 2025
Location: Riverside Park, Middleton (Annapolis County)
Time: 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM
Prizes will be awarded at 12:45 PM. Participants must be present at the event to receive their prize. 


This event is brought to you by Jijuktu’kwejk Watershed Alliance and the Clean Annapolis River Project. 

The Jijuktu’kwejk Watershed Alliance was formed in 2016 to restore and protect the ecological health of the Jijuktu’kwejk (Cornwallis) River watershed in Nova Scotia, through science, leadership, and community engagement.  We are a charitable, non-governmental community-based organization:  As a group of scientists, students, volunteers, organizations and individuals, we are pooling our combined knowledge, skills and experiences to make the Jijuktu’kwejk River a cleaner, safer, more beautiful river.

Founded in 1990, Clean Annapolis River Project (CARP) is a charitable, community-based, non-governmental organization committed to enhancing the ecological health of the Annapolis River watershed through science, leadership and community engagement. CARP's work includes Species at Risk research, water quality monitoring, habitat and in-stream restoration, property assessments for Beneficial Management Practices, agricultural stewardship programs, and more!

Inventory of species found during MST fall walks and forays in 2025

https://inaturalist.ca/pages/help-inaturalist-canada-en#geoprivacy). Observations without obscured locations will be removed.

title: Limestone Barrens of Newfoundland
description: Gather species diversity of northern and southern Limestone Barrens on the island of Newfoundland. We welcome diversity of all organisms!
